EN.title: Encryption software
EN.body:
## Overview

VaultWares encryption software provides comprehensive data protection through full-disk encryption (FDE), file-level encryption, and folder encryption. Protect data at rest on workstations, servers, and removable media with military-grade AES-256 encryption.

## Product editions

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Standard" icon="lock">
    **Use case**: Individual workstations  
    **Encryption**: Full-disk, file, folder  
    **Management**: Local only
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Professional" icon="shield">
    **Use case**: Small business  
    **Encryption**: Full-disk, file, folder, removable media  
    **Management**: Centralized console
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Enterprise" icon="building">
    **Use case**: Large organizations  
    **Encryption**: All types + cloud storage  
    **Management**: Enterprise console + API
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Server" icon="server">
    **Use case**: Server environments  
    **Encryption**: Boot volumes, data volumes  
    **Management**: Headless operation
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Key features

### Encryption capabilities
- **Full-disk encryption**: Encrypt entire system drives including boot sectors
- **File-level encryption**: Selective encryption of individual files
- **Folder encryption**: Automatic encryption of designated folders
- **Removable media**: Encrypt USB drives and external storage
- **Cloud storage**: Encrypt files before uploading to cloud services
- **Email encryption**: Integrate with email clients for encrypted messages

### Security features
- **AES-256-XTS encryption**: Military-grade encryption standard
- **Pre-boot authentication**: Secure boot process before OS loads
- **Multi-factor authentication**: Password + TPM + biometric + smart card
- **Self-encrypting drive (SED) support**: Hardware-accelerated encryption
- **Secure key management**: TPM-based key storage
- **Emergency recovery**: Secure key escrow and recovery procedures

### Management features
- **Centralized console**: Manage encryption across entire organization
- **Policy enforcement**: Automated encryption policies
- **Compliance reporting**: Detailed encryption status reports
- **Remote management**: Deploy, configure, and monitor remotely
- **Active Directory integration**: User and group policy management
- **Audit logging**: Complete encryption operation logs

## System requirements

### Minimum requirements
- **OS**: Windows 10/11, Windows Server 2016+, macOS 11+, Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+, RHEL 8+)
- **CPU**: 2 GHz dual-core processor
- **RAM**: 4GB (8GB recommended)
- **Disk space**: 500MB for software, additional space for encrypted containers
- **TPM**: TPM 1.2 or 2.0 (recommended for full-disk encryption)

### Supported platforms
- Physical machines
- Virtual machines (VMware, Hyper-V, KVM)
- Cloud instances (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Containers (encryption of persistent volumes)

## Installation

### Windows installation

<Steps>
  <Step title="Download installer">
    Download VaultCrypt from the [customer portal](https://portal.vaultwares.com).
  </Step>
  <Step title="Run installer">
    ```powershell
    # Silent installation
    VaultCrypt-Setup.exe /S /LICENSE=your-license-key
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Reboot system">
    Restart your computer to complete installation.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Configure encryption">
    Launch VaultCrypt and follow the encryption wizard.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### macOS installation

```bash
# Install via Homebrew
brew tap vaultwares/tap
brew install vaultcrypt

# Or download DMG
curl -O https://downloads.vaultwares.com/vaultcrypt-latest.dmg
open vaultcrypt-latest.dmg
```

### Linux installation

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Debian/Ubuntu">
    ```bash
    # Add repository
    wget -qO - https://repo.vaultwares.com/gpg.key | sudo apt-key add -
    echo "deb https://repo.vaultwares.com/apt stable main" | \
      sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vaultwares.list
    
    # Install
    sudo apt update
    sudo apt install vaultcrypt
    
    # Activate license
    sudo vaultcrypt-cli activate --license your-license-key
    ```
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="RHEL/CentOS">
    ```bash
    # Add repository
    sudo wget https://repo.vaultwares.com/rpm/vaultwares.repo \
      -O /etc/yum.repos.d/vaultwares.repo
    
    # Install
    sudo yum install vaultcrypt
    
    # Activate license
    sudo vaultcrypt-cli activate --license your-license-key
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Usage

### Full-disk encryption

<CodeGroup>
```powershell Windows
# Encrypt system drive
vaultcrypt-cli encrypt-disk `
  --drive C: `
  --algorithm aes-256-xts `
  --auth-method password+tpm `
  --recovery-key-file C:\recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
vaultcrypt-cli status C:
```

```bash macOS
# Encrypt system volume
sudo vaultcrypt encrypt-volume \
  --volume / \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-method password+filevault \
  --recovery-key-file ~/recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
vaultcrypt status /
```

```bash Linux
# Encrypt root partition
sudo vaultcrypt encrypt-disk \
  --device /dev/sda1 \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-method password+tpm \
  --recovery-key-file /root/recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
sudo vaultcrypt status /dev/sda1
```
</CodeGroup>

### File and folder encryption

```bash
# Encrypt a file
vaultcrypt encrypt-file \
  --input sensitive-document.pdf \
  --output sensitive-document.pdf.enc \
  --password "your-password"

# Decrypt a file
vaultcrypt decrypt-file \
  --input sensitive-document.pdf.enc \
  --output sensitive-document.pdf \
  --password "your-password"

# Create encrypted folder
vaultcrypt create-vault \
  --path ~/SecureDocuments \
  --size 10GB \
  --password "your-password"

# Mount encrypted folder
vaultcrypt mount ~/SecureDocuments

# Unmount encrypted folder
vaultcrypt unmount ~/SecureDocuments
```

### Removable media encryption

```bash
# Encrypt USB drive
vaultcrypt encrypt-removable \
  --device /dev/sdb1 \
  --label "Secure USB" \
  --password "your-password" \
  --portable-mode enable

# Portable mode allows the drive to be used on systems
# without VaultCrypt installed (includes portable app)
```

## Enterprise management

### Deploy via Group Policy (Windows)

```powershell
# Create GPO for VaultCrypt deployment
New-GPO -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" | New-GPLink -Target "OU=Workstations,DC=example,DC=com"

# Configure encryption policy
Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "RequireEncryption" -Type DWord -Value 1

Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "EncryptionAlgorithm" -Type String -Value "AES-256-XTS"

Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "RequireTPM" -Type DWord -Value 1
```

### Centralized management console

```bash
# Connect to management server
vaultcrypt-admin connect \
  --server mgmt.example.com \
  --username admin \
  --password "admin-password"

# Deploy encryption policy
vaultcrypt-admin policy deploy \
  --name "Corporate Encryption Policy" \
  --targets "OU=Workstations" \
  --require-fde true \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-methods password,tpm \
  --compliance-check daily

# Generate compliance report
vaultcrypt-admin report generate \
  --type compliance \
  --format pdf \
  --output compliance-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).pdf
```

### API integration

```python
from vaultwares import VaultCryptAPI

# Initialize API client
api = VaultCryptAPI(
    server='https://mgmt.example.com',
    api_key='your-api-key'
)

# Get encryption status for all devices
devices = api.devices.list()
for device in devices:
    status = api.devices.get_encryption_status(device.id)
    print(f"{device.hostname}: {status.encryption_percentage}% encrypted")

# Deploy encryption policy
policy = api.policies.create(
    name='New Hire Encryption',
    require_fde=True,
    algorithm='aes-256-xts',
    auth_methods=['password', 'tpm']
)

# Apply policy to device group
api.policies.apply(policy.id, group='new-hires')
```

## Recovery procedures

### User-initiated recovery

```bash
# Generate recovery key during encryption
vaultcrypt encrypt-disk \
  --drive C: \
  --recovery-key-file recovery-key.txt \
  --recovery-key-print

# User can recover using recovery key at pre-boot screen
# Enter recovery key when prompted
```

### Administrator recovery

```bash
# Connect to management console
vaultcrypt-admin connect --server mgmt.example.com

# Retrieve recovery key for user
vaultcrypt-admin recovery get-key \
  --user john.doe \
  --device LAPTOP-12345 \
  --reason "User forgot password" \
  --approver admin@example.com

# Reset user password
vaultcrypt-admin recovery reset-password \
  --user john.doe \
  --device LAPTOP-12345 \
  --new-password "temporary-password"
```

### Emergency access

```bash
# Create emergency access disk
vaultcrypt-admin emergency-disk create \
  --output emergency-access.iso \
  --include-recovery-keys \
  --authorized-admins admin1,admin2

# Boot from emergency disk to access encrypted system
```

## Performance optimization

### Hardware acceleration

```bash
# Enable AES-NI acceleration (Intel/AMD CPUs)
vaultcrypt config set --hardware-acceleration enable

# Use self-encrypting drives (SED)
vaultcrypt config set --sed-support enable --opal-version 2.0

# Check performance
vaultcrypt benchmark
# Output:
# Encryption speed: 2.5 GB/s
# Decryption speed: 2.8 GB/s
# CPU overhead: 3%
```

### Optimization settings

```bash
# Optimize for performance
vaultcrypt config optimize --profile performance

# Optimize for security
vaultcrypt config optimize --profile security

# Custom optimization
vaultcrypt config set \
  --cache-size 256MB \
  --io-priority high \
  --background-conversion true
```

## Compliance and auditing

### Generate compliance reports

```bash
# Encryption compliance report
vaultcrypt-admin report compliance \
  --standard pci-dss \
  --output pci-compliance-report.pdf

# Audit log export
vaultcrypt-admin audit export \
  --start-date 2024-01-01 \
  --end-date 2024-01-31 \
  --format json \
  --output audit-log.json
```

### Compliance standards supported

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="PCI DSS">
    Meets PCI DSS Requirement 3.4 for rendering PAN unreadable through encryption.
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="HIPAA">
    Satisfies HIPAA Security Rule addressable implementation specification for encryption of ePHI.
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="GDPR">
    Provides technical measures for data protection as required by GDPR Article 32.
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="FIPS 140-2">
    Uses FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules for encryption operations.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Troubleshooting

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Encryption is slow">
    - Enable hardware acceleration: `vaultcrypt config set --hardware-acceleration enable`
    - Check CPU usage during encryption
    - Consider using SED drives for better performance
    - Schedule encryption during off-hours
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Cannot boot after encryption">
    - Boot from recovery media
    - Enter recovery key at pre-boot screen
    - Check BIOS/UEFI settings (Secure Boot, TPM)
    - Contact support with error code
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="TPM errors">
    - Clear TPM in BIOS/UEFI
    - Reinitialize TPM: `vaultcrypt tpm-init`
    - Update TPM firmware
    - Use password-only authentication as fallback
  </Accordion>
  
  <Accordion title="Performance degradation">
    - Check disk health: `vaultcrypt disk-health`
    - Verify sufficient free space (20% minimum)
    - Disable real-time antivirus scanning of encrypted volumes
    - Update VaultCrypt to latest version
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Next steps

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Best practices" icon="star" href="/guides/best-practices">
    Encryption best practices
  </Card>
  <Card title="API reference" icon="code" href="/api-reference/endpoints">
    Programmatic management
  </Card>
  <Card title="Compliance" icon="shield-check" href="/compliance/gdpr">
    Compliance guides
  </Card>
  <Card title="Support" icon="headset" href="/support/contact">
    Get technical assistance
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

QC.title: Logiciels de chiffrement
QC.description: Solutions de chiffrement de disque entier et de fichiers
QC.body:
## Aperçu

Le logiciel de chiffrement VaultWares offre une protection complète des données grâce au chiffrement de disque entier (CDE), au chiffrement au niveau des fichiers et au chiffrement des dossiers. Protégez les données au repos sur les postes de travail, les serveurs et les médias amovibles avec le chiffrement AES-256 de qualité militaire.

## Éditions du produit

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Standard" icon="lock">
    **Cas d'utilisation** : Postes de travail individuels
    **Chiffrement** : Disque entier, fichier, dossier
    **Gestion** : Local uniquement
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Professional" icon="shield">
    **Cas d'utilisation** : Petites entreprises
    **Chiffrement** : Disque entier, fichier, dossier, médias amovibles
    **Gestion** : Console centralisée
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Enterprise" icon="building">
    **Cas d'utilisation** : Grandes organisations
    **Chiffrement** : Tous types + stockage cloud
    **Gestion** : Console d'entreprise + API
  </Card>
  <Card title="VaultCrypt Server" icon="server">
    **Cas d'utilisation** : Environnements serveurs
    **Chiffrement** : Volumes de démarrage, volumes de données
    **Gestion** : Opération sans interface graphique
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Fonctionnalités clés

### Capacités de chiffrement
- **Chiffrement de disque entier** : Chiffre les disques système entiers, y compris les secteurs de démarrage
- **Chiffrement au niveau des fichiers** : Chiffrement sélectif de fichiers individuels
- **Chiffrement des dossiers** : Chiffrement automatique des dossiers désignés
- **Médias amovibles** : Chiffre les clés USB et les supports de stockage externes
- **Stockage cloud** : Chiffre les fichiers avant de les téléverser vers des services cloud
- **Chiffrement des courriels** : Intégration avec les clients de messagerie pour des messages chiffrés

### Fonctionnalités de sécurité
- **Chiffrement AES-256-XTS** : Norme de chiffrement de qualité militaire
- **Authentification pré-démarrage** : Processus de démarrage sécurisé avant le chargement du système d'exploitation
- **Authentification multi-facteurs** : Mot de passe + TPM + biométrique + carte à puce
- **Support de disque à chiffrement intégré (SED)** : Chiffrement accéléré par le matériel
- **Gestion sécurisée des clés** : Stockage des clés basé sur le TPM
- **Récupération d'urgence** : Procédures de séquestre et de récupération des clés sécurisées

### Fonctionnalités de gestion
- **Console centralisée** : Gérez le chiffrement dans toute l'organisation
- **Application des politiques** : Politiques de chiffrement automatisées
- **Rapports de conformité** : Rapports détaillés sur l'état du chiffrement
- **Gestion à distance** : Déployer, configurer et surveiller à distance
- **Intégration Active Directory** : Gestion des utilisateurs et des groupes
- **Journalisation d'audit** : Journaux complets des opérations de chiffrement

## Exigences système

### Exigences minimales
- **OS** : Windows 10/11, Windows Server 2016+, macOS 11+, Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+, RHEL 8+)
- **CPU** : Processeur dual-cœur de 2 GHz
- **RAM** : 4 Go (8 Go recommandé)
- **Espace disque** : 500 Mo pour le logiciel, espace supplémentaire pour les conteneurs chiffrés
- **TPM** : TPM 1.2 ou 2.0 (recommandé pour le chiffrement de disque entier)

### Plateformes prises en charge
- Machines physiques
- Machines virtuelles (VMware, Hyper-V, KVM)
- Instances cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Conteneurs (chiffrement des volumes persistants)

## Installation

### Installation Windows

<Steps>
  <Step title="Télécharger l'installateur">
    Téléchargez VaultCrypt depuis le [portail client](https://portal.vaultwares.com).
  </Step>
  <Step title="Exécuter l'installateur">
    ```powershell
    # Silent installation
    VaultCrypt-Setup.exe /S /LICENSE=your-license-key
    ```
  </Step>
  <Step title="Redémarrer le système">
    Redémarrez votre ordinateur pour terminer l'installation.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Configurer le chiffrement">
    Lancez VaultCrypt et suivez l'assistant de chiffrement.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Installation macOS

```bash
# Install via Homebrew
brew tap vaultwares/tap
brew install vaultcrypt

# Or download DMG
curl -O https://downloads.vaultwares.com/vaultcrypt-latest.dmg
open vaultcrypt-latest.dmg
```

### Installation Linux

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Debian/Ubuntu">
    ```bash
    # Add repository
    wget -qO - https://repo.vaultwares.com/gpg.key | sudo apt-key add -
    echo "deb https://repo.vaultwares.com/apt stable main" | \
      sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vaultwares.list

    # Install
    sudo apt update
    sudo apt install vaultcrypt

    # Activate license
    sudo vaultcrypt-cli activate --license your-license-key
    ```
  </Tab>

  <Tab title="RHEL/CentOS">
    ```bash
    # Add repository
    sudo wget https://repo.vaultwares.com/rpm/vaultwares.repo \
      -O /etc/yum.repos.d/vaultwares.repo

    # Install
    sudo yum install vaultcrypt

    # Activate license
    sudo vaultcrypt-cli activate --license your-license-key
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Utilisation

### Chiffrement de disque entier

<CodeGroup>
```powershell Windows
# Encrypt system drive
vaultcrypt-cli encrypt-disk `
  --drive C: `
  --algorithm aes-256-xts `
  --auth-method password+tpm `
  --recovery-key-file C:\recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
vaultcrypt-cli status C:
```

```bash macOS
# Encrypt system volume
sudo vaultcrypt encrypt-volume \
  --volume / \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-method password+filevault \
  --recovery-key-file ~/recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
vaultcrypt status /
```

```bash Linux
# Encrypt root partition
sudo vaultcrypt encrypt-disk \
  --device /dev/sda1 \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-method password+tpm \
  --recovery-key-file /root/recovery-key.txt

# Check encryption status
sudo vaultcrypt status /dev/sda1
```
</CodeGroup>

### Chiffrement de fichiers et de dossiers

```bash
# Encrypt a file
vaultcrypt encrypt-file \
  --input sensitive-document.pdf \
  --output sensitive-document.pdf.enc \
  --password "your-password"

# Decrypt a file
vaultcrypt decrypt-file \
  --input sensitive-document.pdf.enc \
  --output sensitive-document.pdf \
  --password "your-password"

# Create encrypted folder
vaultcrypt create-vault \
  --path ~/SecureDocuments \
  --size 10GB \
  --password "your-password"

# Mount encrypted folder
vaultcrypt mount ~/SecureDocuments

# Unmount encrypted folder
vaultcrypt unmount ~/SecureDocuments
```

### Chiffrement des médias amovibles

```bash
# Encrypt USB drive
vaultcrypt encrypt-removable \
  --device /dev/sdb1 \
  --label "Secure USB" \
  --password "your-password" \
  --portable-mode enable

# Portable mode allows the drive to be used on systems
# without VaultCrypt installed (includes portable app)
```

## Gestion d'entreprise

### Déploiement via Stratégie de groupe (Windows)

```powershell
# Create GPO for VaultCrypt deployment
New-GPO -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" | New-GPLink -Target "OU=Workstations,DC=example,DC=com"

# Configure encryption policy
Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "RequireEncryption" -Type DWord -Value 1

Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "EncryptionAlgorithm" -Type String -Value "AES-256-XTS"

Set-GPRegistryValue -Name "VaultCrypt Deployment" `
  -Key "HKLM\Software\VaultWares\VaultCrypt\Policy" `
  -ValueName "RequireTPM" -Type DWord -Value 1
```

### Console de gestion centralisée

```bash
# Connect to management server
vaultcrypt-admin connect \
  --server mgmt.example.com \
  --username admin \
  --password "admin-password"

# Deploy encryption policy
vaultcrypt-admin policy deploy \
  --name "Corporate Encryption Policy" \
  --targets "OU=Workstations" \
  --require-fde true \
  --algorithm aes-256-xts \
  --auth-methods password,tpm \
  --compliance-check daily

# Generate compliance report
vaultcrypt-admin report generate \
  --type compliance \
  --format pdf \
  --output compliance-report-$(date +%Y%m%d).pdf
```

### Intégration API

```python
from vaultwares import VaultCryptAPI

# Initialize API client
api = VaultCryptAPI(
    server='https://mgmt.example.com',
    api_key='your-api-key'
)

# Get encryption status for all devices
devices = api.devices.list()
for device in devices:
    status = api.devices.get_encryption_status(device.id)
    print(f"{device.hostname}: {status.encryption_percentage}% encrypted")

# Deploy encryption policy
policy = api.policies.create(
    name='New Hire Encryption',
    require_fde=True,
    algorithm='aes-256-xts',
    auth_methods=['password', 'tpm']
)

# Apply policy to device group
api.policies.apply(policy.id, group='new-hires')
```

## Procédures de récupération

### Récupération initiée par l'utilisateur

```bash
# Generate recovery key during encryption
vaultcrypt encrypt-disk \
  --drive C: \
  --recovery-key-file recovery-key.txt \
  --recovery-key-print

# User can recover using recovery key at pre-boot screen
# Enter recovery key when prompted
```

### Récupération administrateur

```bash
# Connect to management console
vaultcrypt-admin connect --server mgmt.example.com

# Retrieve recovery key for user
vaultcrypt-admin recovery get-key \
  --user john.doe \
  --device LAPTOP-12345 \
  --reason "User forgot password" \
  --approver admin@example.com

# Reset user password
vaultcrypt-admin recovery reset-password \
  --user john.doe \
  --device LAPTOP-12345 \
  --new-password "temporary-password"
```

### Accès d'urgence

```bash
# Create emergency access disk
vaultcrypt-admin emergency-disk create \
  --output emergency-access.iso \
  --include-recovery-keys \
  --authorized-admins admin1,admin2

# Boot from emergency disk to access encrypted system
```

## Optimisation des performances

### Accélération matérielle

```bash
# Enable AES-NI acceleration (Intel/AMD CPUs)
vaultcrypt config set --hardware-acceleration enable

# Use self-encrypting drives (SED)
vaultcrypt config set --sed-support enable --opal-version 2.0

# Check performance
vaultcrypt benchmark
# Output:
# Encryption speed: 2.5 GB/s
# Decryption speed: 2.8 GB/s
# CPU overhead: 3%
```

### Paramètres d'optimisation

```bash
# Optimize for performance
vaultcrypt config optimize --profile performance

# Optimize for security
vaultcrypt config optimize --profile security

# Custom optimization
vaultcrypt config set \
  --cache-size 256MB \
  --io-priority high \
  --background-conversion true
```

## Conformité et audit

### Générer des rapports de conformité

```bash
# Encryption compliance report
vaultcrypt-admin report compliance \
  --standard pci-dss \
  --output pci-compliance-report.pdf

# Audit log export
vaultcrypt-admin audit export \
  --start-date 2024-01-01 \
  --end-date 2024-01-31 \
  --format json \
  --output audit-log.json
```

### Normes de conformité prises en charge

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="PCI DSS">
    Satisfait l'exigence 3.4 de PCI DSS pour rendre le PAN illisible par chiffrement.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="HIPAA">
    Satisfait la spécification d'implémentation applicable de la règle de sécurité HIPAA pour le chiffrement de l'ePHI.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="GDPR">
    Fournit des mesures techniques pour la protection des données comme l'exige l'article 32 du RGPD.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="FIPS 140-2">
    Utilise des modules cryptographiques validés FIPS 140-2 pour les opérations de chiffrement.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Dépannage

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Le chiffrement est lent">
    - Activer l'accélération matérielle : `vaultcrypt config set --hardware-acceleration enable`
    - Vérifier l'utilisation du CPU pendant le chiffrement
    - Envisager d'utiliser des disques SED pour de meilleures performances
    - Planifier le chiffrement pendant les heures creuses
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Impossible de démarrer après le chiffrement">
    - Démarrer à partir d'un média de récupération
    - Entrer la clé de récupération à l'écran pré-démarrage
    - Vérifier les paramètres du BIOS/UEFI (Secure Boot, TPM)
    - Contacter le support avec le code d'erreur
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Erreurs TPM">
    - Effacer le TPM dans le BIOS/UEFI
    - Réinitialiser le TPM : `vaultcrypt tpm-init`
    - Mettre à jour le micrologiciel TPM
    - Utiliser l'authentification par mot de passe uniquement en dernier recours
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Dégradation des performances">
    - Vérifier l'état du disque : `vaultcrypt disk-health`
    - Vérifier l'espace libre suffisant (minimum 20 %)
    - Désactiver l'analyse antivirus en temps réel des volumes chiffrés
    - Mettre à jour VaultCrypt vers la dernière version
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>
